representation: One allocation contains distinct payload and replicated control regions; only FULL slots contain live payloads.
admission: A same-root same-version FULL control observation admits equality and payload access; EMPTY terminates the probe.
owner_traffic: No payload owner moves; hash and equality receive borrows only.
failure_cleanup: Callback abort and miss preserve table state; mutation invalidates occupancy and probe facts.
provenance: Returned buckets and borrows remain rooted in the table allocation, not the hash or integer index.
concurrency: No shared concurrency in the audited route.
reference_events: Existing group loads, tag tests, equality on matching FULL slots, probe advance, and EMPTY stop only.
forbidden_delta: No extra payload initialization, load, check, scan, allocation, indirection, atomic, metadata field, or owner movement.
unresolved: Exact group-operation semantics remain a separate machine-leaf obligation; no generated code was inspected.
falsifier: Reject if control metadata can authorize EMPTY or DELETED payload, or if admission needs Hashbrown recognition or a new runtime check.

representation: EMPTY or DELETED control selects uninitialized payload storage; growth may replace the whole physical root.
admission: A version-bounded vacant token admits exactly one commit to the corresponding payload place.
owner_traffic: Offered key and value remain caller-owned until commit, then each moves once; the result borrows the new value.
failure_cleanup: Precommit callback or allocation failure preserves or returns owners; abandonment after opening the slot requires fixed closure without a scan or temporary tag.
provenance: Vacant and result tokens are tied to the exact table root and version; growth re-roots results and invalidates old tokens.
concurrency: No shared concurrency in the audited route.
reference_events: Probe, optional one growth allocation, reference relocation, one control update, one payload write, and exact counter changes.
forbidden_delta: No zero fill, dummy payload, second allocation, extra owner move, rollback scan, persistent tag, or result indirection.
unresolved: Exact allocation leaf and formal transition safety remain unproved.
falsifier: Reject if metadata becomes FULL before any rule prevents access to unwritten payload, or if failure loses an offered owner.

representation: One FULL slot remains occupied while its value owner changes; callback replacement may temporarily remove the whole item.
admission: The matching occupied-entry token admits one exact replace or replace/remove protocol.
owner_traffic: New value moves in, old value moves out, stored key stays, and the duplicate offered key is destroyed exactly once; callback branches retain one item owner.
failure_cleanup: Equality abort changes nothing; callback success, remove, abort, and abandonment need branch-exact disposition and a valid final table state.
provenance: Returned old value is owned; any new borrow derives from the same live slot under the new mutation version.
concurrency: No shared concurrency in the audited route.
reference_events: One value replacement or one take plus optional restore; no allocation on the reference path.
forbidden_delta: No key move, clone, zeroing, extra tag, allocation, scan, or persistent protocol state.
unresolved: Callback-abort cleanup and formal effect rules remain unproved.
falsifier: Reject if a copied pointer can duplicate the temporarily extracted owner or any callback branch lacks one exact disposition.

representation: A FULL payload slot becomes EMPTY or DELETED; group-local state chooses whether probe reachability needs a tombstone.
admission: A same-root occupied-entry token admits moving the exact payload owner out before metadata ceases to authorize access.
owner_traffic: Stored key-value ownership moves once to the result; no in-place payload destruction occurs.
failure_cleanup: Miss changes nothing; found removal has no recoverable failure after transfer; consumed entry guards and all derived borrows invalidate.
provenance: The result is owned and the guard-to-slot relation is root- and version-bounded.
concurrency: No shared concurrency in the audited route.
reference_events: One owner read, group-local emptiness tests, one control update, item decrement, and conditional growth counter update.
forbidden_delta: No zeroing, payload copy, allocation, whole-table scan, tag, atomic, or extra owner movement.
unresolved: Exact EMPTY-versus-DELETED proof and fact invalidation remain paper obligations.
falsifier: Reject if the removed payload remains accessible or lookup correctness requires an added scan or metadata bit.

representation: Resize uses old and new sparse roots; in-place rehash temporarily interprets DELETED as an unprocessed live payload inside a non-escapable phase.
admission: Only the rehash phase may admit transition-local DELETED-live payload; ordinary sparse access may not.
owner_traffic: Every live item has exactly one owner; resize relocates once, while in-place branches restore, relocate, or swap one displaced owner until placed.
failure_cleanup: Resize allocation or hashing failure preserves old owners and releases raw new storage; in-place partial progress needs exact processed and unprocessed cleanup.
provenance: Resize re-roots all results; in-place results retain the physical root but receive new versioned occupancy facts.
concurrency: No shared concurrency in the audited route.
reference_events: Reference branch selection, one allocation only for resize, reference scans and relocations, or one in-place control scan and displacement loop.
forbidden_delta: No zero fill, second payload copy, extra allocation, persistent phase tag, whole-table rollback copy, or extra scan beyond the selected algorithm.
unresolved: Formal partial-progress cleanup and exact allocation/group leaves remain unproved.
falsifier: Reject if ordinary DELETED grants payload access, a phase can escape, or failure duplicates, drops, or leaks a displaced owner.

representation: A page is partitioned into blocks; a free block initializes only an intrusive next word and the remaining payload bytes may be uninitialized.
admission: Thread-local page authority plus a non-null free head admits popping exactly one block.
owner_traffic: The page transfers one raw-block owner to the caller; no payload owner is copied or relocated.
failure_cleanup: Fast commit cannot fail after the pop; slow allocation may collect, extend, retry once, or return null without returning a block owner.
provenance: The returned block is rooted in the physical page allocation, not separate metadata, heap association, or address-map slot.
concurrency: Fast allocation is thread-local and uses no atomic operation.
reference_events: Direct size-class page load, one head test, next load, head store, used increment, one internal-pointer clearing store, and return.
forbidden_delta: No whole-block initialization, permanent slot tag, per-block backpointer, atomic, scan, synchronization, extra allocation, payload copy, or owner relocation on the fast path.
unresolved: Exact release assembly and timing were not inspected; configured debug, secure, tracking, guarded, and zeroing paths differ.
falsifier: Reject if safe uninitialized payload requires zeroing or a tag, or if returning one block loses its page-root identity.

representation: A live caller block changes role to a local intrusive free node in the same physical slot.
admission: The unique block owner and matching page-thread authority admit non-atomic local free-list and used-count mutation.
owner_traffic: The caller owner moves once into page.local_free; typed destruction must finish before the raw block transfer.
failure_cleanup: Checks precede mutation; valid local linking cannot fail; rare empty-page retirement or full-page queue transition has exact page disposition.
provenance: The allocation pointer maps to the physical page root; queue or heap movement does not change that root.
concurrency: Same-thread authority permits non-atomic local updates.
reference_events: Address-to-page lookup, thread-ID test, intrusive link write, local-head store, used decrement, and rare retire or unfull transition.
forbidden_delta: No atomic push, fence, global lock, added registry lookup, scan, zeroing, fill, per-block tag, or extra owner move.
unresolved: Configured diagnostics and instrumentation vary; no generated code was inspected.
falsifier: Reject if local free pays remote synchronization or if payload remains live after its storage becomes free-list metadata.

representation: The freed block overlays its payload with an intrusive link; atomic xthread_free combines list head and page-ownership state.
admission: Before successful CAS the remote caller retains the block; successful CAS atomically transfers it and may claim an abandoned page.
owner_traffic: One block owner moves to the remote list, later to local_free and free; collection decrements used once and final release consumes the page root only after all blocks return.
failure_cleanup: Weak CAS may retry; detach and collection traverse at most capacity; abandoned-page handling chooses release, reclaim, re-advertise, or unown; corrupt unsafe state is not an admissible result.
provenance: A live block keeps constant page data and the physical page root alive across separate metadata and heap reassociation; final release unregisters the map first.
concurrency: Remote publication and capture use acquire-release CAS; page dispatch is relaxed; abandoned-map readers use a source-specific quiescence protocol.
reference_events: Address lookup, thread branch, link write, one successful per-page CAS plus retries, deferred bounded traversal, splice, count update, and conditional lifecycle work.
forbidden_delta: No per-block count, hazard slot, epoch record, global lock, immediate full scan, extra allocation, backpointer, permanent tag, or mandatory synchronization beyond source events.
unresolved: Source memory-order TODOs, weak-CAS progress, bitmap busy-wait progress, and platform page-map proofs remain open.
falsifier: Reject if reclamation policy is baked into the language, ownership transfers before successful CAS, or final release can race a live block or map reader.

representation: A page byte root contains header, cell-pointer array, gap, variable cell ranges, freeblocks, and optional overflow; scratch tails may be uninitialized.
admission: Initialized MemPage metadata and bounds admit exact cell subranges; writeability is published only after old bytes are journaled.
owner_traffic: Page pins, overflow pages, cell bytes, and scratch roots move through a bounded balance; allocation failure may leave a rollback-required database rather than return every change locally.
failure_cleanup: Local failure releases every acquired pin and scratch allocation, but balance failure may leave topology inconsistent and requires transaction rollback; depth is capped at twenty and sibling sets are fixed.
provenance: Cell pointers may root in cache pages or several scratch roots; page numbers and cursor slots are logical identities only; subrange invalidation preserves nonoverlapping bytes.
concurrency: BtShared mutex, write transaction, table lock, and pager file policy serialize this slice; no lock-free atomics are present.
reference_events: Exact cell copies, overflow-page allocation, journal-before-write, fixed sibling scans, page allocation or freeing, rekeying, pointer-map work, and bounded scratch allocation.
forbidden_delta: No unused-tail zeroing, cell tag, per-cell allocation, whole-page copy from coarse borrowing, whole-tree scan, extra snapshot, lock, atomic, fence, owner move, or loss of fast insertion shape.
unresolved: Higher-level proof that every balancing error reaches rollback was outside scope; formal byte-range safety is unproved.
falsifier: Reject if rollback-required state can escape, scratch provenance is lost, or whole-root exclusivity forces a copy that precise subranges avoid.

representation: A removed cell range becomes page free space; stale bytes remain unless secure-delete policy chooses zeroing; predecessor cells can carry overflow-chain ownership across page roots.
admission: Valid cursor, cell index, initialized page metadata, exact bounds, journaled writeability, and no conflicting reader admit deletion.
owner_traffic: Overflow pages transfer to the database freelist; predecessor cell plus overflow relation moves without duplication; unused sibling pages transfer to freelist; pins are released exactly.
failure_cleanup: Failure may occur after frees, removal, transfer, or sibling rewrites; local cleanup releases pins and scratch while pager rollback repairs database state; traversal is bounded by payload length and tree depth.
provenance: Cell pointers root in pinned page buffers or scratch, not page numbers; secure delete and freeblock overlay have different exact invalidation footprints.
concurrency: Mutex, transaction, table lock, and no-conflicting-reader policy apply; no language reclamation policy is implied.
reference_events: Native overflow-chain traversal, freelist updates, fixed sibling rebalance, pins, journaling, optional pointer map, and policy-selected zeroing.
forbidden_delta: No unconditional zeroing or destructor traversal, duplicate overflow owner, cell box or tag, whole-tree scan, coarse-borrow page copy, extra disk write, per-mutation undo closure, lock, atomic, fence, or metadata.
unresolved: Typed affine row cleanup and higher-level rollback enforcement were outside this byte-record slice.
falsifier: Reject if overflow ownership duplicates during predecessor transfer or if local restoration is mandatory and adds undo work absent from SQLite.

representation: Pager states own database, journal or WAL, cache pages, bitsets, savepoints, locks, and one reusable page scratch buffer.
admission: Writer state plus journal-before-write admits mutable pages; rollback playback is admitted only in a writer or hot-recovery state with required locks.
owner_traffic: Old page images reside in journal or WAL rather than per-mutation closures; replay overwrites existing cache roots, invalidates derived metadata, finalizes resources, and returns to reader state on success.
failure_cleanup: Journal offsets advance monotonically; malformed tail has defined stop behavior; I/O or allocation failure enters persistent PAGER_ERROR and does not finalize the journal; later recovery may finish.
provenance: Journal page numbers are logical selectors; physical roots are cache allocations, scratch, file storage, MemPage extras, and backup destinations; overwrite invalidates every old derived fact.
concurrency: VFS locks, pager state, and WAL writer policy serialize recovery; rollback-journal and WAL are library-selected policies.
reference_events: Native first-write journal records, checksums and syncs, sequential replay or WAL undo, dirty-list traversal, one scratch page, reinitialization callback, and journal finalization.
forbidden_delta: No extra snapshot, inverse closure, graph node, allocation, second pass, tag, unrelated zeroing, double old/new retention, sync, lock, atomic, fence, fixed journal policy, or writer theorem.
unresolved: VFS and WAL internals, crash proof, exact filesystem events, and fail-closed reinitializer behavior remain UNKNOWN.
falsifier: Reject if failed playback can expose a stable pager, if every mutation needs local inverse cleanup, or if one journal or WAL policy becomes language semantics.

representation: Atomic and Shared are word-sized tagged addresses; Guard points to a stable participant Local allocation in a collector domain.
admission: First pin publishes a pinned epoch and barrier; Atomic.load borrows the guard lifetime but performs no runtime collector, validity, publication, or interference check.
owner_traffic: Pin creates one scoped guard; load moves no pointee owner and returns a copyable observer; last guard unpins.
failure_cleanup: No recoverable failure; lazy registration may allocate; abandonment drops the guard only.
provenance: Payload borrows root in the pointee allocation, while guard and collector roots are separate; Atomic stores no collector identity.
concurrency: Pin and unpin establish reclamation observation with architecture-specific barriers; caller-selected pointer ordering handles publication; payload interference is separate.
reference_events: Existing first-pin counter and barrier work, infrequent cold collection, and exactly one caller-selected atomic load per protected load.
forbidden_delta: No per-load allocation, count, hazard publication, collector lookup, dynamic root check, extra fence, indirection, or metadata field.
unresolved: Same-domain association, portable x86 fence justification, pointer validity, publication, and payload interference remain external source obligations.
falsifier: Reject if guard presence alone grants dereference or a B route adds a per-load event beyond the source atomic load.

representation: A successful unlink yields a retired allocation; one-shot deferred records are three inline words plus a call pointer; queue sentinel payload is uninitialized by topology role.
admission: Checked successful structural unlink must derive the unique retirement right; mere Copy Shared address and epoch guard are insufficient.
owner_traffic: Failed CAS returns the offered owner; successful unlink retains one disposition right, moves any payload result once, and transfers the retired root into one deferred record consumed at destruction.
failure_cleanup: Full local bag moves as a sealed owner to the global queue then retries the still-owned action; execution may be delayed forever; arbitrary callback termination, panic, and recursive effects are UNKNOWN.
provenance: Target allocation identity survives movement through Local bag and global queue node and remains associated with the collector domain.
concurrency: Unlink ordering is data-structure policy; bag sealing and later grace period provide reclamation; callbacks may run on another thread.
reference_events: Existing unlink atomics and retry, one inline deferred record, amortized queue-node allocation and sealing fence only when a bag flushes.
forbidden_delta: No per-retire allocation on the inline path, count, object epoch, hazard record, second owner box, tracing scan, pointer hop, extra atomic, or fence.
unresolved: Exactly-once retirement, unreachability, collector association, and arbitrary callback cleanup are external unsafe Rust obligations.
falsifier: Reject if a copied address can retire, unreachability is a writer assertion, or the route adds per-retire structural events.

representation: Each Local has a live-prefix bag of 64 Deferred slots; sealed bags add one epoch and move through a sentinel queue; only prefix elements are initialized.
admission: A bag is eligible two epoch advances after sealing; advance requires every pinned participant to record the current epoch; stalled or older participants fail closed.
owner_traffic: Eligible pop moves one sealed bag; Bag drop replaces each active entry with NO_OP before one-shot execution; queue pop retires its old sentinel and can create later deferred work.
failure_cleanup: Ordinary collection attempts at most eight pops and may make no progress; callbacks can add work, panic, or not terminate; eventual execution is not promised.
provenance: Participant, collector, queue bag, and each target allocation are distinct roots; disposition stays tied to its target while eligibility stays tied to the collector.
concurrency: Participant scan, fences, epoch advance, queue atomics, and two-epoch expiry implement Crossbeam policy; payload synchronization is separate.
reference_events: One participant scan per advance attempt, at most eight queue removals, active-prefix callback traversal, existing bag allocation and queue events, and cold code shape.
forbidden_delta: No lock, background thread, full queue scan, object epoch, eager slot zeroing, all-64 traversal, extra grace pass, atomic, fence, tracing indirection, or stronger progress work.
unresolved: Arbitrary callback termination/effects, eventual reclamation, stalled traversal, shutdown drain, and portable pin barrier remain UNKNOWN.
falsifier: Reject if epoch becomes the only language policy, sentinel liveness needs a queue form, or the route adds policy-independent runtime tax.
